player.text: Cup-headed character with blue body and white head. In gameplay, occupies roughly 5-8% of frame width. Can appear smaller on world map or zoomed scenes. Up to 2 players possible (co-op: Cuphead + Mugman).
Player sprite changes with state: standing, ducking (shorter/wider), dashing (stretched horizontal), jumping (airborne), dead (collapsed). Box must match the current sprite shape, not a fixed size.

enemy.text: Any hostile or interactive non-player character. Includes: bosses (large, often 25-50% of frame), minions (small, similar size to player), and world map NPCs. Size varies enormously — box must match the actual sprite regardless of size.

Run-and-gun levels have 5 enemy movement patterns: stationary ground, path-following, shooting (fires projectiles), big (larger hitbox), and spawner (creates other enemies). Visual size and shape varies by pattern.

16 unique enemy types plus bosses. Boss enemies can have multiple phases where they transform into completely different visual forms mid-fight. Annotate the current visible form, not what it was before.

projectile.text: Any object fired, thrown, or hazardous. Always smaller than the entity that produced it. Includes bullets, fireballs, energy beams, falling objects. If it moves and can damage the player, it's a projectile.

96 unique projectile types across 36 levels. Each level has completely different projectile visuals (bullets, fireballs, tears, gems, hearts, gumballs, pitchforks, arrows, etc). The model must generalize across all of them.
Boss levels average 3-5 distinct projectile types per fight. Devil has 9 types. Run-and-gun levels have 1-3 types. Expect multiple different projectiles in the same frame.

platform.text: Solid surface the player can stand on. Typically wider than tall. Only appears in platforming/run-and-gun levels, never in boss arenas or menus. Box should cover the entire walkable surface.
Platforms only exist in run-and-gun and platforming levels (8 levels). Boss arenas are flat ground — do not annotate the floor as a platform. Flying levels have no platforms at all.

boss_fight.text: One large enemy dominates the frame. Multiple projectiles likely. Player is usually present. No platforms unless it's a platforming boss.

run_and_gun.text: Side-scrolling level with multiple small enemies, projectiles, and platforms. Player moves left to right. More objects per frame than boss fights.

world_map.text: Isometric overworld. Player is small. Other characters are NPCs (annotate as enemy). Mission nodes are stone pedestals. No projectiles or platforms.

flying_level.text: Airplane/flying levels (Airplane, AirshipClam, AirshipCrab, etc). Player is in a plane, not on foot. No platforms. Enemies and projectiles approach from the right. Player sprite is the plane, not Cuphead.

dice_palace.text: Dice Palace has 12 sub-levels, each a mini-boss. Small arenas, unique enemy per room. Fewer projectiles than main bosses.

tightness.text: Boxes must tightly enclose the visible sprite with zero padding. Every edge of the box should touch the edge of the object.

proportions.text: Use relative sizing: projectiles should be smaller than the player. If a 'projectile' box is larger than the player box in the same frame, it is likely mislabeled or the box is too loose.

completeness.text: Annotate ALL visible instances of every class. Do not skip small or partially occluded objects. Partial visibility still gets a box covering the visible portion.

one_box_per_object.text: Each distinct object gets exactly one box. Do not split a single enemy into multiple boxes. Do not merge two adjacent enemies into one box.
